title: W4: passive tools logger — PostToolUse, fail-open, zero enforcement, fails-when-broken tests
action: Per plan.md 'Slice 3A shape settled at planning (2026-07-17)' and AO15 (decision f1ca79b9): add the passive tools logger. (1) hooks/bee-tools-logger.mjs — thin wrapper on the bee-prompt-context.mjs pattern: readHookContext('tools-logger') via adapter; no root or missing lib -> exit 0; hookEnabled(root,'tools-logger') gate; then append ONE JSON line to .bee/logs/tools.jsonl: {ts, tool_name, agent_id: payload.agent_id ?? null, agent_type: payload.agent_type ?? null} — orchestrator calls carry NO agent fields (AO15), log null; include duration/status fields ONLY if the payload carries them; NEVER log tool_input/tool_response bodies (PII/secret discipline). Everything fail-open: every throw -> logCrash + exit 0; never stderr, never block, never a decision — zero enforcement is the contract. mkdirSync recursive on .bee/logs like bee-model-guard's logDispatch. (2) hooks/catalog.mjs — PostToolUse entry, BOTH runtimes, NO matcher (= every tool; renders like the matcher-less UserPromptSubmit entry), cmd('bee-tools-logger.mjs','bee: tools logger'). hooks/hooks.json and hooks/claude-hooks.json are BYTE-CHECKED against renderProjection by the contract suite — regenerate them by running renderProjectionText from the catalog (there is NO standalone render CLI; run it via node -e or a scratch script and paste byte-exact; the drift rows self-correct you). .codex/hooks.json is DIFFERENT (panel BLOCKER): it is a HAND-CURATED development/fallback snapshot (test_hook_contracts.mjs:773, D9) that deliberately omits SubagentStart/codex-audit — do NOT re-render it (that would inject ~20 unrelated lines); hand-add ONLY the PostToolUse tools-logger command, copying the shape of the existing PostToolUse state-sync group at .codex/hooks.json:38-49. (3) KNOWN RED you must fix: test_hook_contracts.mjs:812 asserts repoCommands.length === 11 ('expected 11' at :824) in the required codex-repo-target-generated-topology row — the new BOTH-runtimes entry makes it 12; update the count (and its message) as part of the change, it is not an unexplained failure. (4) .claude/settings.json — add the PostToolUse group (no matcher) running node "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"/.bee/bin/hooks/bee-tools-logger.mjs alongside the existing state-sync group (which keeps its TaskCreate|TaskUpdate|TodoWrite matcher); settings.json is not byte-checked, hand-add is safe. (5) skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/state.mjs — DEFAULT_HOOKS gains 'tools-logger': true (~line 97-104); mirror .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs byte-identical (no DEFAULT_HOOKS census exists in test_lib.mjs — panel verified; no deviation needed). (6) hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs — add 'bee-tools-logger.mjs' to WRAPPERS (~198; adversarial malformed-stdin rows auto-apply) PLUS the fails-when-broken pair reusing the suite's buildFixture/runWrapper/readHooksJsonl patterns: happy-path row (fixture root, real PostToolUse payload with tool_name and no agent fields -> assert the tools.jsonl line exists with tool_name + null attribution; second payload WITH agent_id/agent_type -> both logged verbatim) and crash-injection row (broken state.mjs fixture: existsSync passes, import throws -> assert exit 0 AND a visible crash line in .bee/logs/hooks.jsonl — fail-open never fail-silent). Mirror .bee/bin/hooks/bee-tools-logger.mjs byte-identical. Notes for the report, not code: per-tool-call node spawn is precedented (write-guard fires per Edit/Bash/Read); tools.jsonl has no rotation, consistent with hooks.jsonl/dispatch.jsonl. No claim anywhere that this reduces cost — it measures (CONTEXT ban). FINAL STEP: node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write, then the full verify — --check green.

truths: a PostToolUse payload produces exactly one tools.jsonl line: ts, tool_name, agent_id/agent_type (null when absent — orchestrator calls), no tool_input/tool_response content ever
the wrapper exits 0 on every input — malformed stdin, broken lib, unwritable log — and a crash leaves a visible line in .bee/logs/hooks.jsonl (fail-open, never fail-silent)
the happy-path test FAILS if the logger stops writing lines (fails-when-broken, W4 standing trap)
hooks/hooks.json + hooks/claude-hooks.json regenerated byte-exact from the catalog; .codex/hooks.json hand-extended with ONLY the tools-logger PostToolUse group (no SubagentStart/audit injection)
codex-repo-target-generated-topology count updated 11 -> 12 with its message
artifacts: a sample tools.jsonl line from the happy-path fixture quoted in the report
prohibitions: no enforcement of any kind: no deny, no stderr verdicts, no turn control
no tool_input/tool_response bodies in the log
no re-render of .codex/hooks.json (hand-curated snapshot, D9) — hand-add the one group only
no edits outside the bounded files (release-manifest.json only via release_manifest.mjs --write)

verify: node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node hooks/test_model_guard.mjs && node hooks/test_write_guard.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check

trace.outcome: W4 passive tools logger shipped: hooks/bee-tools-logger.mjs (PostToolUse, no matcher, BOTH runtimes, fail-open) appends {ts, tool_name, agent_id, agent_type} to .bee/logs/tools.jsonl per AO15/f1ca79b9; zero enforcement, no tool_input/tool_response bodies ever. catalog.mjs gained the entry; hooks.json/claude-hooks.json regenerated byte-exact; .codex/hooks.json hand-extended with only the tools-logger group (D9 snapshot, no re-render); .claude/settings.json hand-added; DEFAULT_HOOKS['tools-logger']=true in both state.mjs copies. Fixed the KNOWN RED (repoCommands 11->12) plus an independently-found sibling instance (.codex/hooks.json hand-count 9->10). Added the fails-when-broken pair (happy-path x2 + crash-injection) plus malformed-input auto-apply and 4 new repo-route arms. Full verify chain green; release manifest regenerated and checked.
